description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring a set of rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a low-profile base. The panels are arranged in a single, slightly tilted row, optimized for sunlight capture. The base structure appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing stability and durability. Several colored components, including red and green elements, suggest the presence of electrical junction boxes or control units integrated into the system. The installation is designed for ground placement, suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility areas. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed panel surfaces and visible support framework.
description: A compact solar energy installation with a single row of tilted photovoltaic panels on a sturdy metal base, featuring colored electrical components and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the solar panels, the tilted angle, and colored electrical components at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the installation and the arrangement of the panels in a single row.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backside of the panels, support structure, and additional electrical components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the panel tilt and the compact base structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a low-profile aluminum or steel frame
